issue.message: Class referenced in the manifest, `leakcanary.internal.activity.LeakLauncherActivity`, was not found in the project or the libraries
Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background

Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background

issue.message: Consider using `apply()` instead; `commit` writes its data to persistent storage immediately, whereas `apply` will handle it in the background

Call requires API level 36 (current min is 26): `java.lang.Thread#threadId`
Call requires API level 30 (current min is 26): `android.os.storage.StorageVolume#getDirectory`
Call requires API level 35 (current min is 26): `java.util.List#removeFirst` (Prior to API level 35, this call would resolve to a Kotlin stdlib extension function. You can use `removeAt(0)` instead.)

issue.message: Call requires API level 35 (current min is 26): `java.util.List#removeFirst` (Prior to API level 35, this call would resolve to a Kotlin stdlib extension function. You can use `removeAt(0)` instead.)

issue.errorLine1: targetSdk = 35 // 保持35以避免Android 16的新运行时行为

issue.message: Do not hardcode "`/data/`"; use `Context.getFilesDir().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "`/data/`"; use `Context.getFilesDir().getPath()` instead
Attribute `enableOnBackInvokedCallback` is only used in API level 33 and higher (current min is 31)

This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. If you do not want clicked URLs to bring the user to your app, remove the `android.intent.category.BROWSABLE` category, or set `android:autoVerify="false"` to make it clear this is not intended to be an Android App Link.

This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. If you do not want clicked URLs to bring the user to your app, remove the `android.intent.category.BROWSABLE` category, or set `android:autoVerify="false"` to make it clear this is not intended to be an Android App Link.

This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. If you do not want clicked URLs to bring the user to your app, remove the `android.intent.category.BROWSABLE` category, or set `android:autoVerify="false"` to make it clear this is not intended to be an Android App Link.

This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. If you do not want clicked URLs to bring the user to your app, remove the `android.intent.category.BROWSABLE` category, or set `android:autoVerify="false"` to make it clear this is not intended to be an Android App Link.
Avoid passing `null` as the view root (needed to resolve layout parameters on the inflated layout's root element)

Creating a MutableState object with a mutable collection type
Switch statement on an `int` with known associated constant missing case `Player.STATE_BUFFERING`, `Player.STATE_IDLE`
Content padding parameter paddingValues is not used

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).
Missing x86_64 ABI support for ChromeOS
Using `setJavaScriptEnabled` can introduce XSS vulnerabilities into your application, review carefully
`checkClientTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers
issue.errorLine1: override fun checkClientTrusted(chain: Array<out X509Certificate>?, authType: String?) {}

issue.message: `checkServerTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers
issue.errorLine1: override fun checkServerTrusted(chain: Array<out X509Certificate>?, authType: String?) {}

issue.message: `checkClientTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers
`checkServerTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers

Implementing a custom `X509TrustManager` is error-prone and likely to be insecure. It is likely to disable certificate validation altogether, and is non-trivial to implement correctly without calling Android's default implementation.
Exported service does not require permission
Insecure Base Configuration
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is never < 26

Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is never < 26

This folder configuration (`v26`) is unnecessary; `minSdkVersion` is 26. Merge all the resources in this folder into `mipmap-anydpi`.
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `DanmakuManager` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `MiniPlayerManager` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
This field leaks a context object

Reading a value annotated with @FrequentlyChangingValue inside composition
Reading a value annotated with @FrequentlyChangingValue inside composition
Reading a value annotated with @FrequentlyChangingValue inside composition
Very long vector path (12973 characters), which is bad for performance. Considering reducing precision, removing minor details or rasterizing vector.

Prefer `mutableIntStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`

Prefer `mutableFloatStateOf` instead of `mutableStateOf`
Reading `value` will cause an autoboxing operation. Use `floatValue` to avoid unnecessary allocations.
Assigning `value` will cause an autoboxing operation. Use `floatValue` to avoid unnecessary allocations.

Assigning `Locale.getDefault()` to a final static field is suspicious; this code will not work correctly if the user changes locale while the app is running
issue.errorLine1: private val dateFormat = SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS", Locale.getDefault())

issue.message: Assigning `Locale.getDefault()` to a final static field is suspicious; this code will not work correctly if the user changes locale while the app is running
